tactical_shape: notional 3-4-2-1, often resembling a 5-4-1 with two banks sitting deep
compact team that absorbed pressure and looked to counter through possession
standout_players: Eldor Shomurodov did a lot of chasing
Abbosbek Fayzullaev nodded in the equaliser from close range
home_read.momentum: They stayed in the game until the end, but the punditry suggests beating stronger sides will be difficult if they remain mostly defensive.

tactical_shape: dominated possession for most of the game
sideways passing early on before improving after the hydration break
dropped deep after regaining the lead and invited pressure
standout_players: Luis Díaz – man of the match, provided a goal and an assist
Daniel Muñoz – scored a brilliant opening goal
Jefferson Lerma – controlling figure in midfield
Camilo Vargas – partly at fault for Uzbekistan's equaliser after the ball deflected off his thighs
Gustavo Puerta – laid on Díaz for the winning goal
Jaminton Campaz – sealed the win with a header in added time
Juan Camilo Hernández – tenacious work helped create the third goal
away_read.momentum: A winning start puts Colombia in charge of the group, though the punditry says they still need to be more clinical and may be hard to judge until tested by a more proactive opponent.
